name: USS Cony Sonar Shack
description: A below-decks compartment directly beneath the bridge, housing the SQS-4 sonar console and its operator. The room is cooled by the ship's air conditioning — a luxury that seems obscene given what is happening sixty meters below the hull — and lit by the blue-white glow of the sonar display.
atmosphericDetails: The SQS-4 display shows the contact in crisp, high-resolution detail — the submarine's exact bearing, estimated depth, and the absence of any propulsion noise for the last ninety minutes.
A paper printout from the sonar recorder is accumulating on the deck — a continuous strip of acoustic data that constitutes the most complete record of the B-59's behavior tonight.
A second display, smaller and to the left, shows the task force's own depth-charge detonations as brief, bright spikes — each one labeled by time and bearing.
